title: Imitation Learning
summary: A type of learning where an agent learns to mimic the behavior of another agent or expert. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Learning Techniques. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into Imitation Learning Agent and Multi-Agent Imitation Learning.

related: RLHF Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback

blocks.title: Overview
blocks.body: A type of learning where an agent learns to mimic the behavior of another agent or expert. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Learning Techniques. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into Imitation Learning Agent and Multi-Agent Imitation Learning.

blocks.title: Compare and Contrast

panels.label: What it is
panels.body: A type of learning where an agent learns to mimic the behavior of another agent or expert.

panels.label: What it is not
panels.body: It is not just Inverse Reinforcement Learning and Forward and Inverse Reinforcement Learning; the graph shows how it sits inside Reinforcement Learning.

panels.label: Common confusion
panels.body: Ask whether the term changes policy, reward, exploration, or control dynamics. Compare it against Inverse Reinforcement Learning and Forward and Inverse Reinforcement Learning so the boundary stays visible instead of collapsing into a synonym pile.

panels.label: Best next step
panels.body: Review Q-Learning and V-Learning first, then move toward Imitation Learning Agent and Multi-Agent Imitation Learning.

blocks.takeaway: The comparison view keeps Imitation Learning honest by showing the nearest neighbors, the real boundary, and the next study move.
